---
title: "Next-Best-View Planning with Foundation Models"
subtitle: "Current docs entrypoint for Aria-NBV"
author: "Jan Duchscherer, Munich University of Applied Sciences"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
---

# Abstract

This repository studies quality-driven next-best-view planning for egocentric
indoor scenes. The current stack combines oracle RRI supervision from ASE
ground-truth meshes with a frozen EVL backbone and a compact VINv3 scorer that
ranks candidate views by predicted reconstruction improvement rather than pure
coverage.

The docs site is intentionally pruned around current surfaces. The paper remains
the highest-level project truth, the implementation pages document the live
codebase, and [Ideas and Future Directions](contents/ideas.qmd) is the living
future-work page.

# Start Here

## Highest-level project truth

- [Seminar Project Paper](typst/paper/main.pdf)
- [Typst paper source](typst/paper/main.typ)
- [Outlook presentation](typst/slides/slides_thesis_outlook.pdf)

## Current architecture

- [aria_nbv Overview & Architecture](contents/impl/aria_nbv_overview.qmd)
- [Repository Structure](contents/repo_structure.qmd)
- [ASE Data Pipeline & Streamlit Usage](contents/impl/data_pipeline_overview.qmd)
- [aria_nbv Implementation Overview](contents/impl/oracle_rri_impl.qmd)
- [Computing RRI with Ground Truth Meshes](contents/impl/rri_computation.qmd)

## Current model and training docs

- [View Introspection Network (VIN) on EVL](contents/impl/vin_nbv.qmd)
- [CORAL for Ordinal Regression](contents/impl/coral_intergarion.qmd)
- [API Reference](reference/index.qmd)

## Current direction

- [Ideas and Future Directions](contents/ideas.qmd)
- [Current Priorities](contents/todos.qmd)
- [Project Status](contents/roadmap.qmd)

## Setup and resources

- [Setup Instructions](contents/setup.qmd)
- [Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset](contents/ase_dataset.qmd)
- [Resources & Tools](contents/resources.qmd)
- [Glossary](contents/glossary.qmd)

## Supporting notes

- [Research Questions](contents/questions.qmd)
- [Experimental Findings](contents/experiments/findings.qmd)

# What is intentionally de-emphasized

- Old phase-by-phase roadmap material was collapsed into a short status page.
- The legacy `aria_nbv Package` page is kept only as a compatibility stub.
- Historical VIN v2 proposal pages are still available but are not treated as
  current implementation docs.

# Notes on scope

- `docs/typst/paper/main.typ` remains the authoritative narrative surface when
  Quarto summaries drift.
- Implementation-heavy Quarto pages describe the current repo-owned code and use
  source anchors to map prose back to `aria_nbv/`.
- `ideas.qmd` is the preferred place for forward-looking scratchpad material;
  it is more current than the old roadmap/TODO surfaces.
